body.title: Per-ticket, builder-namespaced branches (<ticket>.<builder>); branches + merger station are a matched pair, triggered by the 2nd live builder; retires CC-straight-to-main

validity_conditions.note: the merger station is this model's consumer; if the pipeline decision is superseded the matched-pair premise changes

body.text:
# D-branching-model-2026-07-08
**title:** Per-ticket, builder-namespaced branches; branches + merger station a matched pair; retires CC-straight-to-main
**date:** 2026-07-08
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-consequence-D-branching-model

## Decision narrative
Now that development can have more than one builder (CC + Aider + DS via D-dev-pipeline-stations), the single-writer 'everyone commits to main' model breaks: concurrent writers collide, and — worse — an unexamined builder's code landing on main violates separation-of-powers (the builder grading itself into trunk, CP6). Builder output must be QUARANTINED until an independent station validates it. A branch (or diff) is that quarantine.

**Granularity — per-ticket, NOT per-builder.** The ticket is already our atom of proof (proof binds to the impl commit), blame (produced_by is per-ticket), and dispatch — the branch is the same atom. A per-ticket branch lets the merger validate+land ONE ticket's diff atomically; a failed exam rejects exactly one ticket, contaminating nothing; short-lived branches barely drift. Per-BUILDER long-lived branches were REJECTED: they serialize a builder's tickets onto one branch (ticket B builds on ticket A's unvalidated work), muddy blame, and drift from main.

**Naming vs isolation are orthogonal.** Naming = `<ticket-id>.<builder>` (Akien's ordering: ticket first as the atom, builder a suffix-qualifier), a FLAT dot-namespace (a `/` would create a refs/heads subdir; `.` keeps it flat + greppable), short-lived — created at build-start, deleted at merge. Isolation = each concurrent builder in its own git WORKTREE so checkouts don't stomp. Examples: `T-granny-dispatch-observability-gap.aider`, `T-foo.ds`, `T-bar.cc`. No practical name-length limit (git bound is ~255 bytes/component; longest realistic name ~41 chars); the constraints are character rules (no space, no `~^:?*[\`, no `..`, no trailing `.lock`).

**MATCHED PAIR — the timing invariant.** Per-ticket branches with no merger station to land them = an accumulating off-main pile (the exact 'pile' a prior session confabulated). So branches and the merger station SHIP TOGETHER, triggered by the same event: the SECOND concurrent builder going live and producing mergeable work (Aider revived / DS on free inference). Until that trigger, stay MAIN-ONLY — per-ticket branches for a single writer are pure ceremony with zero isolation payoff.

**Transition retires 'CC commits straight to main.'** By homogeneity (feedback_homogeneity_over_special_case), once multi-builder is live EVERY builder — including CC — goes branch -> tester -> examiner -> merger -> main. If Aider branches but CC keeps committing to main, CC self-grades and the separation-of-powers invariant breaks for CC's own work. feedback_commit_to_main_directly already encodes 'main while single-writer'; this decision NAMES that rule's exit condition.

**Implementation:** no immediate build ticket — the model's implementation IS the gated merger/tester pipeline (D-dev-pipeline-stations; T-tester-rackmount is its first build). This decision is the governing record that pipeline design cites for branch naming + isolation.

## Hypothesis
When the second concurrent builder goes live, every ticket builds on a `<ticket>.<builder>` branch in an isolated worktree and lands on main only through the merger station, with zero direct-to-main builder commits — so no builder, including CC, grades its own work into trunk.

## Measurement Signal
At the trigger: `git branch` shows per-ticket `<ticket>.<builder>` branches during active builds and none linger post-merge; every non-design commit on main carries a merger-emitted merge-proof; zero builder commits straight to main while multi-builder is active. vs today: single writer commits straight to main after every ticket.

## Intention
I intend that every builder's output is quarantined on its own per-ticket branch and reaches main only through independent validation, so no builder — including CC — grades its own work into trunk.
